docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
This site contains all instructions for accessing and navigating the SSCC 2026 Quantum Chemistry hands‑on sessions.

⚙️ Computing environment

All hands‑on work is carried out using CSC Jupyter for Courses. Quantum chemistry calculations use ORCA or CP2K, depending on the session. No local installation is required.

docs.title: Hands‑on material
docs.text: The practical exercises are delivered as Jupyter notebooks maintained in separate repositories. This page serves only as a starting point and signpost.

docs.title: Electronic structure and molecular simulation with CP2K
docs.text:
➡️ https://github.com/csc-training/sscc-cp2k

For details on how the sessions relate to each other, see the Workflow page. ``

docs.title: Molecular structure building with Avogadro2
docs.text:
For visual inspection and editing of molecular structures we use Avogadro2 via the Mahti Open OnDemand (OOD) service.

Avogadro2 is used only as a graphical molecule editor. All quantum chemistry calculations are performed in Jupyter notebooks using ORCA or CP2K.

docs.title: Accessing Avogadro2 on Mahti
docs.text:
Avogadro2 is provided via a container installation available in the Mahti OOD environment.

  1. Log in to Mahti Open OnDemand https://www.mahti.csc.fi
  2. Start an Interactive Desktop session
  3. Open a terminal inside the desktop session
  4. Enable and load the Avogadro2 module:

```bash module use /projappl/project_2017403/modules module load avogadro2

docs.title: Working environment
docs.text:
The SSCC 2026 Quantum Chemistry hands‑on uses CSC Jupyter for Courses.

All hands‑on work is carried out in this environment. No local installation or software setup is required.

docs.title: Access
docs.text:
• Log in using your CSC or Haka account
  • Ensure multi‑factor authentication (MFA) is activated

docs.title: During the session
docs.text:
1. Open the SSCC 2026 QC course in CSC Jupyter for Courses
  2. Launch the provided notebook environment
  3. Open the notebooks indicated during the session

All required software, including ORCA and CP2K (where applicable), is preconfigured in the environment.

docs.title: Hands‑on workflow
docs.text:
The SSCC 2026 Quantum Chemistry hands‑on consists of several notebook‑based sessions, each focusing on a different topic or methodology.

This page describes how the sessions fit together conceptually. Detailed, step‑by‑step instructions are provided directly in the notebooks.

docs.title: Core Quantum Chemistry
docs.text:
Instructor: Antti Karttunen QC engine: ORCA

This session introduces basic electronic structure calculations and ground‑state workflows using ORCA.

docs.title: Potential Energy Surface exploration
docs.text:
Instructor: Nanna QC engine: ORCA

This session focuses on exploring potential energy surfaces, structural variations, and interpretation of PES‑related results.

docs.title: Multireference methods
docs.text:
Instructor: Nino Runeberg QC engine: ORCA

This session introduces multireference electronic structure methods and discusses when and why they are needed.

docs.title: Electronic structure and molecular simulation with CP2K
docs.text:
Maintainer: CSC Training QC engine: CP2K

This session covers electronic structure and molecular simulation workflows using CP2K.

docs.title: Relationship between sessions
docs.text:
• Each session uses its own set of Jupyter notebooks
  • Sessions can be followed independently, unless stated otherwise during the course
  • Different QC engines (ORCA and CP2K) are used in different sessions; the required environment is provided in CSC Jupyter for Courses
